response: [The indigenous community member is an expert in tracking elephant herds.] [They have spent many years studying the behavior and movement of elephants.] [Today, they are observing a group of elephants in a nearby reserve.] [They have noticed that three different herds have come together to drink at the waterhole.] [This is a rare occurrence, as elephants usually travel in smaller groups or separate herds.] [The first herd consists of 12 elephants.] [This is the smallest of the three herds that have gathered.] [The second herd is twice as large as the first herd.] [This means the second herd has twice the number of elephants as the first herd.] [To calculate the size of the second herd, we multiply the number of elephants in the first herd by two.] [12 multiplied by two equals 24.] [Therefore, the second herd has 24 elephants.] [The third herd is 5 elephants smaller than the second herd.] [This means we need to subtract 5 from the number of elephants in the second herd.] [24 minus 5 equals 19.] [So, the third herd has 19 elephants.] [Now, we need to find the total number of elephants at the waterhole.] [To do this, we add the number of elephants in all three herds together.] [The first herd has 12 elephants.] [The second herd has 24 elephants.] [The third herd has 19 elephants.] [Adding these numbers together gives us the total.] [12 plus 24 equals 36.] [Then, we add the 19 elephants from the third herd.] [36 plus 19 equals 55.] [Therefore, the total number of elephants at the waterhole is 55.] [This is a significant gathering of elephants.] [It is unusual for such a large number of elephants to gather at one waterhole.] [The indigenous community member is watching carefully to observe the behavior of the elephants.] [They are noting how the elephants interact with each other.] [They are also observing how the elephants drink from the waterhole.] [The elephants are moving slowly and calmly.] [They are not showing any signs of stress or aggression.] [The waterhole is a vital source of water for the elephants.] [It is especially important during the dry season.] [The indigenous community member knows that the elephants rely on waterholes for
